name: Phong MAT
description: The Phong MAT creates a material using the Phong Shading model. It has support for textures, reflections, bumps, cone lights, rim lights, alpha maps and more. You can output its GLSL shader into two DATs for further adaptation in a GLSL MAT by using the Output Shader parameter.
summary: The Phong MAT creates a material using the Phong Shading model. It has support for textures, reflections, bumps, cone lights, rim lights, alpha maps and more. You can output its GLSL shader into two D

parameters.label: Ambient uses Diffuse

parameters.name: Specular
parameters.label: Specular
parameters.description:
spec -  - The color of the specular light reflected from the material. This changes the color of the highlights on shiney objects.

 specr - specg - specb -

parameters.name: Emit
parameters.label: Emit
parameters.description:
emit -  - This is the color that the material will emit even if there is no light.

parameters.name: Constant
parameters.label: Constant
parameters.description:
constant -  - Adds to the final color. Where there are point colors, finalcolor +=  Color * Constant Color. This behaves like there is ambient illumination of 1 1 1. It is not affected by textures or transparency.

 constantr - constantg - constantb -

parameters.name: Shininess
parameters.label: Shininess
parameters.description: shininess - Controls the specular highlights (glossyness) of an object. Higher settings are more glossy, like plastic or shiny metal. Lower settings give more of a matte finish.

parameters.name: Color Map
parameters.label: Color Map
parameters.description:
colormap -  - Specifies a  texture that is multiplied by the results of all of the lighting calculations. The alpha of this map is used as a part of calculating the objects alpha.  Clicking on the arrows to the right of the map field will open the Texture Sampling Parameters for Color Map.  The other Map parameters below will have their own Texture Sampling Parameters as well.

  colormapextendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  colormapextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  colormapextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  colormapfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  colormapanisotropy -  -

 uv0 - uv1 - uv2 - uv3 - uv4 - uv5 - uv6 - uv7 - screenspace -

  colormapcoordinterp -  - 

 perspectivecorrect - linear -

parameters.name: Normal Map (Bump)
parameters.label: Normal Map (Bump)
parameters.description:
normalmap -  - Uses a Normal Map from TOPs to create a 'bump map' effect. Bump-mapping simulates bumps or wrinkles in a surface to give it a 3D depth effect. Your geometry must have tangent attributes created for this feature to work (T[4]). Create these using the Attribute Create SOP.

  normalmapextendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  normalmapextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  normalmapextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  normalmapfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  normalmapanisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

  normalmapcoord -  - 

 uv0 - uv1 - uv2 - uv3 - uv4 - uv5 - uv6 - uv7 - screenspace -

  normalmapcoordinterp -  - 

 perspectivecorrect - linear -

parameters.name: Bump Scale
parameters.label: Bump Scale
parameters.description: bumpscale - A multiplier for the 'bump effect' created by the Normal Map parameter.

parameters.name: Parallax Scale
parameters.label: Parallax Scale
parameters.description: parallaxscale - Scale value applied to the height map. Can be used to increase or exaggerate the effect.

parameters.name: Parallax Occlusion
parameters.label: Parallax Occlusion
parameters.description: parallaxocclusion - Enables parallax occlusion, an enhancement of the parallax mapping technique used with the height map. Parallax occlusion improves the quality of the texture offsetting in parallax mapping so that the higher parts of the height map appear to occlude the lower parts, giving a better illusion of height.

parameters.name: Displace Vertices
parameters.label: Displace Vertices
parameters.description: displaceverts - When Enable Height Map above is On, setting Displace  to On will enable true displacement mapping where the vertices of the geometry are displaced based on the Height Map texture and the parameters below.

parameters.name: Displace Scale
parameters.label: Displace Scale
parameters.description: displacescale - A multiplier for the displacement amount.

parameters.name: Displace Midpoint
parameters.label: Displace Midpoint
parameters.description: displacemid - Sets the middle point of displacement map as the start position for the displacement effect.

parameters.name: Environment Map
parameters.label: Environment Map
parameters.description:
envmap -  - Uses a  texture to define an environment map for the material. Environment mapping simulates an object reflecting its surroundings. The  defined in this parameter is the texture that will be reflected. The Env Map is added to whatever the normal lighting will be, so to make an object purely reflective turn the Diffuse and Specular parameters to 0. This input expects a sphere map. An example of a sphere map can be found here. This input will also accept a cube map, created with the Cube Map TOP or the Render TOP's Render Cube Map parameter.

  envmapextendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  envmapextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  envmapextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  envmapfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  envmapanisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.label: Environment Map Color
parameters.description:
envmapcolor -  - This color is multiplied with the texture specified by the  parameter above.

parameters.name: Output Shader...
parameters.label: Output Shader...
parameters.description: outputshader - This button will bring up a dialog that will create a GLSL MAT and Text DATs with shader code that matches whatever effect this Phong  is currently creating. Since shaders are dependent on the number and type of lights, it will list some possible different shader choices, based on what lighting configurations have been used in the current system. If no shaders are listed in the dialog, it means no shader has been rendered in the current session of TouchDesigner. Turn on the viewer for the Phong , or setup a render in a Render . That will create/compile some shaders and will cause the list to be populated. For example if you want to see a shader that does shadow mapping, setup a render that does shadow mapping and you will see that come up in the list.

parameters.name: Alpha Map
parameters.label: Alpha Map
parameters.description:
alphamap -  - This map multiplies the alpha of the object. It uses the red channel of the map, other channels are ignored.

  alphamapextendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  alphamapextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  alphamapextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  alphamapfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  alphamapanisotropy -  -

  alphamapcoord -  -

  alphamapcoordinterp -  - 

 perspectivecorrect - linear -

parameters.name: Uniform Alpha
parameters.label: Uniform Alpha
parameters.description: alphamode - Turning this off will make the alpha change depending on orientation of each polygon's normal compared to the camera. Normals that are pointing at the camera will results in the polygon having an alpha of Alpha Front. Normals that are perpendicular to the camera (facing sideways/up/down) will have Alpha Side for their alpha.

parameters.name: Alpha Front
parameters.label: Alpha Front
parameters.description: alphafront - The opacity of the material. This parameter is multiplied by point alpha of the object (as will as any other alpha source).

parameters.name: Alpha Side
parameters.label: Alpha Side
parameters.description: alphaside - This is used for non-uniform alpha. It is the alpha value polygons that are facing away from the camera will get.

parameters.name: Alpha Rolloff
parameters.label: Alpha Rolloff
parameters.description: rolloff - Controls how the alpha changes from Alpha Front to Alpha Side.

parameters.name: Post-Mult Color by Alpha
parameters.label: Post-Mult Color by Alpha
parameters.description: postmultalpha - At the end of all of the calculations, the color (RGB) is multiplied by the calculated alpha. You can stop this from happening by turning off this toggle.

parameters.name: Mult Alpha by Light Luminance
parameters.label: Mult Alpha by Light Luminance
parameters.description: alphamultlight - When this is enabled, the luminance of the lighting will be multiplied by the alpha, to decrease/increase it.

parameters.name: Multi-Texturing (Disables Color Map)

parameters.name: GLSL Expression
parameters.label: GLSL Expression
parameters.description: multitexexpr - GLSL code that combines the texture images (look to the start of this section for more details). This parameter can be left blank (which means the maps will just be multiplied together).

parameters.name: Rim Light
parameters.label: Rim Light
parameters.description: Sequence of rim light info

parameters.name: Enable
parameters.label: Enable
parameters.description: rimlight0enable - Enables this rim light.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
rimlight0color -  - The color of the rim light.

 rimlight0colorr - rimlight0colorg - rimlight0colorb -

parameters.name: Center
parameters.label: Center
parameters.description: rimlight0center - The center of the rim lights location, situated somewhere on a 360 degree circle.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.label: Width
parameters.description: rimlight0width - How far from the center the rim light extends.

parameters.name: Strength
parameters.label: Strength
parameters.description: rimlight0strength - Controls the brightness of the rim light.

parameters.name: Strength Ramp
parameters.label: Strength Ramp
parameters.description: rimlight0strengthramp - You can specify a horizontal ramp (it will sample the texture at v = 0.5), which controls the the rim lights strength.

parameters.name: Shadow Strength
parameters.label: Shadow Strength
parameters.description: shadowstrength - This parameter will control how much being in a shadow will change the color of the lighting. At 1 the object will take on the  parameter, at 0 it will behave as if it's not in a shadow, even if it is.

parameters.name: Shadow Color
parameters.label: Shadow Color
parameters.description:
shadowcolor -  - The color that will be used in shadowed areas.

 shadowcolorr - shadowcolorg - shadowcolorb -

parameters.name: Darkness Emit
parameters.label: Darkness Emit
parameters.description: darknessemit - The Phong  calculates the current brightness of color of the objects, after taking into account lights, rim lights, emission etc. It then uses this brightness (between 0-1) and fades in the . The darker the area, the more of the darkness emit color that will be applied.

parameters.name: Darkness Emit Color
parameters.label: Darkness Emit Color
parameters.description:
darknessemitcolor -  - The color that is used for areas that are in darkness.

 darknessemitcolorr - darknessemitcolorg - darknessemitcolorb -

parameters.name: Secondary Shininess
parameters.label: Secondary Shininess
parameters.description: shininess2 - Controls the secondary specular highlights (glossyness) of an object. Higher settings are more glossy, like plastic or shiny metal. Lower settings give more of a matte finish.

parameters.name: Write Camera Space Depth to Alpha
parameters.label: Write Camera Space Depth to Alpha
parameters.description: writecameradepthtoalpha - This causes the camera space depth of the pixel to be written to the alpha channel of the output . This value can be useful for post-processing effects, but ofcourse you will not have the result of all the alpha calculations if you turn this on (although they'll get used to multiply the output color, assuming  is enabled.

parameters.name: Apply Point Color
parameters.label: Apply Point Color
parameters.description: applypointcolor - Normally the color attribute (Cd[4]) coming from the  is used in the lighting calculation, you can turn off using the color attribute by un-checking this parameter.

parameters.description: zero - one - worldspaceposition - worldspacenormal - cameraspaceposition - cameraspacenormal - pointcolor - texturecoord0 - diffusemap - normalmap - specmap - emitmap - emitcolor - diffcolor - difflighting - finaldiffcolor - speccolor - spec2color - speclighting - spec2lighting - finalspeccolor - shadowstrength - normalizedshadowstrength -

parameters.name: Deform
parameters.label: Deform
parameters.description: dodeform - Enables deforms on this material.

parameters.name: Get Bone Data:
parameters.label: Get Bone Data:
parameters.description:
deformdata -  - Specifies where the deform bone data will be obtained.

 sop - mat - deformin -

parameters.name: From a SOP
parameters.label: From a SOP
parameters.description: sop - mat - deformin -

parameters.name: SOP with Capture Data
parameters.label: SOP with Capture Data
parameters.description: targetsop - Specifies the  that contains the deform capture attributes.

parameters.name: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.label: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.description: pcaptpath - Specifies the name of the pCaptPath attribute to use. When your geometry has been put through a Bone Group SOP, the attributes will be split into names like pCaptPath0, pCaptPath1. You can only render 1 bone group at a time, so this should match the group you are rendering with this material.

parameters.name: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.label: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.description: pcaptdata - Much like pCaptPath Attrib.

parameters.name: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.label: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.description: skelrootpath - Specifies the path to the  where the root of the skeleton is located.

parameters.name: MAT
parameters.label: MAT
parameters.description: mat - When obtaining deform data from a  or a Deform In , this is where that  is specified.

parameters.name: Blending (Transparency)
parameters.label: Blending (Transparency)
parameters.description: blending - This toggle enables and disables blending. However see the wiki article Transparency.

parameters.name: Blend Operation
parameters.label: Blend Operation
parameters.description:
blendop -  - 

 add - subtract - revsubtract - minimum - maximum -

parameters.name: Add
parameters.label: Add
parameters.description: add - subtract - revsubtract - minimum - maximum -

parameters.name: Source Color *
parameters.label: Source Color *
parameters.description:
srcblend -  - This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Color).

 zero - dcol - omdcol - sa - omsa - da - omda - sas - one - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.name: Destination Color *
parameters.label: Destination Color *
parameters.description:
destblend -  - This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Color).

 one - scol - omscol - sa - omsa - da - omda - zero - dcol - omdcol - sas - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.name: One
parameters.label: One
parameters.description: one - scol - omscol - sa - omsa - da - omda - zero - dcol - omdcol - sas - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.name: Separate Alpha Function
parameters.label: Separate Alpha Function
parameters.description: separatealphafunc - This toggle enables and disables separate blending options for the alpha values.

parameters.name: Alpha Blend Operation
parameters.label: Alpha Blend Operation
parameters.description:
blendopa -  - 

 add - subtract - revsubtract - minimum - maximum -

parameters.name: Source Alpha *
parameters.label: Source Alpha *
parameters.description:
srcblenda -  - This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Alpha).

 zero - dcol - omdcol - sa - omsa - da - omda - sas - one - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.name: Destination Alpha *
parameters.label: Destination Alpha *
parameters.description:
destblenda -  - This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Alpha).

 one - scol - omscol - sa - omsa - da - omda - zero - dcol - omdcol - sas - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.label: Blend Constant Color
parameters.description:
blendconstant -  - 

 blendconstantr - blendconstantg - blendconstantb -

parameters.label: Blend Constant Alpha
parameters.description: blendconstanta -

parameters.label: Legacy Alpha Behavior
parameters.description: legacyalphabehavior -

parameters.label: Point Color Pre-Multiply
parameters.description:
pointcolorpremult -  - 

 alreadypremult - premultinshader -

parameters.name: Already Pre-Multiplied By Alpha
parameters.label: Already Pre-Multiplied By Alpha
parameters.description: alreadypremult - premultinshader -

parameters.name: Depth Test
parameters.label: Depth Test
parameters.description: depthtest - Enables and disables the Depth-Test. If the depth-test is disabled, depths values aren't written to the Depth-Buffer.

parameters.name: Depth Test Function
parameters.label: Depth Test Function
parameters.description:
depthfunc -  - The depth value of the pixel being drawn is compared to the depth value currently in the depth-buffer using this function. If the test passes then the pixel is drawn to the -Buffer. If the test fails the pixel is discarded and no changes are made to the -Buffer.

 less - lessorequal - equal - greater - greaterorequal - notequal - always -

parameters.name: Less Than
parameters.label: Less Than
parameters.description: less - lessorequal - equal - greater - greaterorequal - notequal - always -

parameters.name: Write Depth Values
parameters.label: Write Depth Values
parameters.description: depthwriting - If Write Depth Values is on, pixels that pass the depth-test will write their depth value to the Depth-Buffer. If this isn't on then no changes will be made to the Depth-Buffer, regardless of if the pixels drawn pass or fail the depth-test.

parameters.name: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.label: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.description: alphatest - This enables or disables the pixel alpha test.

parameters.name: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.label: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.description:
alphafunc -  - This menu works in conjunction with the Alpha Threshold parameter below in determining which pixels to keep based on their alpha value.

 less - lessorequal - greater - greaterorequal -

parameters.name: Alpha Threshold
parameters.label: Alpha Threshold
parameters.description: alphathreshold - This value is what the pixel's alpha is compared to to determine if the pixel should be drawn. Pixels with alpha greater than the Alpha Threshold will be drawn. Pixels with alpha less than or equal to the Alpha Threshold will not be drawn.

parameters.name: Wire Frame
parameters.label: Wire Frame
parameters.description:
wireframe -  - Enables and disables wire-frame rendering with the option of OpenGL Tesselated or Topology based wireframes.

 off - tesselated - topology -

parameters.name: Line Width
parameters.label: Line Width
parameters.description: wirewidth - This value is the width that the wires will be. This value is in pixels.

parameters.name: Cull Face
parameters.label: Cull Face
parameters.description:
cullface -  - Selects which faces to render.

 userender - Use the render settings found in the Render or Render Pass . neither - Do not cull any faces, render everything. backfaces - Cull back faces, render front faces. frontfaces - Cull front faces, render back faces. bothfaces - Cull both faces, render nothing.

parameters.name: Use Render Setting
parameters.label: Use Render Setting
parameters.description: userender - Use the render settings found in the Render or Render Pass . neither - Do not cull any faces, render everything. backfaces - Cull back faces, render front faces. frontfaces - Cull front faces, render back faces. bothfaces - Cull both faces, render nothing.

parameters.name: Polygon Depth Offset
parameters.label: Polygon Depth Offset
parameters.description: polygonoffset - Turns on the polygon offset feature.

parameters.name: Offset Factor
parameters.label: Offset Factor
parameters.description: polygonoffsetfactor -

parameters.name: Offset Units
parameters.label: Offset Units
parameters.description: polygonoffsetunits -
